name: asm_volatile_scaffolding
title: Per-instruction asm volatile inside C function (NOT a single inline asm block)
proof: func_8007E8DC (commit 106ad5e2) and func_8007F87C (commit 424f89a) -- both display.c functions with hand-tuned scheduling that no pure C structure could reproduce.

structural: Target asm has hand-tuned scheduling pattern (interleaved multu/mflo with specific nop placement, register cycles, etc.)
permuter plateaus high (5000+) on pure C variants
diff shows fundamentally different instruction order than GCC produces

rule_skeleton.instruction: Use `register <type> name asm("$reg")` to pin variables; emit specific instructions via per-instruction `asm volatile`; let surrounding C be regular C. Add `.set noreorder` / `.set reorder` around critical sections via asm volatile.

notes: DO NOT use a single block of `__asm__("long string of instructions")` -- that's effectively renaming INCLUDE_ASM and won't be accepted.
Use per-instruction asm volatile so GCC can interleave C operations and the function reads as legitimate C.
Combine with `--multu-funcs` flag (multu_funcs.txt) for the multu/mult conversion if needed.
Worker for func_8007E8DC also fixed tools/regfix.py idx-0 reorder bug as a side effect -- look for similar tooling-gap opportunities.
